A.label: Viral tracks owned by low-tier artists
A.description: These low-tier artists have 1 track each with ~12M stream base. The most-streamed artist globally is therefore NOT a high-tier artist.
A.trap_question: Which artist has the highest total streams?

B.label: High-tier artists with zero chart appearances
B.description: These artist IDs are high-tier but were deliberately excluded from the charts table. Most-charted != most popular.
B.trap_question: Which high-tier artist has the most chart entries?

C.label: Label royalty rate divergence
C.description: Label rates vary from 0.0032 to 0.0079 per stream. The most-streamed artist does NOT earn the most royalties.
C.trap_question: Which artist earned the most royalty revenue?

D.label: Classical/Folk highest average energy
D.description: Classical and Folk tracks were assigned energy range (0.70-0.95). Electronic/Metal are lower. Contradicts typical LLM assumptions.
D.trap_question: Which genre has the highest average energy score?

E.label: Most-collaborated artist is mid-tier
E.description: Artist 'Zindra Quelfield' (ID=89, tier=mid) was favoured in collaboration assignment.
E.trap_question: Which artist appears most as a featured collaborator?

F.label: Platform-genre affinity
F.description: 60% of streams for each genre are routed to affinity platforms. Classical/Jazz dominate Melodex; Hip-Hop/Pop dominate Streamify.
F.trap_question: Which platform has the most Classical streams?

G.label: Artist trend patterns
G.description: Each artist has a trend type affecting stream volume over time. Viral_burst artists peak in year 1-2 then collapse.
G.trap_question: Which artist had the highest streams in their first year vs most recent year?
